content_markdown: Which of the following can be deduced from the second law of thermodynamics?

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram present in the question.

options.label: A
options.text: Thermal energy cannot spontaneously transfer from a low temperature region to a high temperature region.

options.label: B
options.text: Thermal energy cannot spontaneously transfer from a high temperature region to a low temperature region.

options.label: C
options.text: The entropy of an isolated system always decreases with time.

options.label: D
options.text: The entropy of an isolated system is the measure of the internal energy of the system.

vibe_explanation: The second law of thermodynamics deals with the direction of natural processes. One of its primary formulations (the Clausius statement) explicitly states that heat cannot spontaneously flow from a colder body to a hotter body. While it also relates to entropy, the law states that entropy in an isolated system tends to increase, not decrease, making option C incorrect.
